mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, encouraging risk-taking and inflows into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains below its trailing 12-month average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of low FEDFUNDS show no significant positive return bias (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 across walk-forward windows containing trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in housing starts and retail sales growth indicates a joint weakening of consumer and investment demand that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both HOUST and RSAFS show negative month-over-month momentum concurrently.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM < 0 and RSAFS MoM < 0 concurrently
failure_condition: Joint negative-momentum episodes fail to show significantly more negative 20-day forward returns than single-series negative episodes (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle economic expansion, sustained industrial production growth reflects healthy corporate output and supports forward earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when INDPRO year-over-year growth remains positive and the economy is not near a monetary tightening peak.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: During periods of positive INDPRO growth in mid-cycle regimes, 60-day forward returns are not significantly greater than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Continued monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in sustained economic momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following flat/negative prints (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation accelerates above 3.5%, markets price in expectations of future monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation episodes fail to show significantly more negative 20-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or fewer than 55% of triggered instances are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and a sharp decline in housing starts jointly signal an approaching demand-side recession that individually weaker signals do not capture, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter when GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth falls below 2% while HOUST declines more than 10% over 3 months.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2% and HOUST 3-month decline > 10%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not produce significantly more negative 60-day forward returns than episodes triggered by either series alone (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by historically low unemployment and accommodative interest rates, further tightening of the labor market (falling UNRATE) is interpreted by markets as a late-cycle warning sign of overheating rather than strength, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, in contrast to the positive relationship observed in earlier expansion phases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5% and FEDFUNDS below 12-month trailing average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly negative relative to baseline expansion-phase returns (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC flips sign across more than half of rolling 2-year windows.
